name: GPT-4o
archDisplay: Transformer (architecture undisclosed); natively multimodal single model
note: Natively multimodal 'omni' flagship; the chat-completions API serves text+vision, with audio native to the underlying model exposed via separate Realtime/Audio endpoints.

results.notes: OpenAI-reported figure, mirrored on this community thread since the original openai.com post blocks automated fetching [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

OpenAI-reported figure, mirrored on this community thread since the original openai.com post blocks automated fetching [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

OpenAI-reported figure, mirrored on this community thread since the original openai.com post blocks automated fetching [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

Multilingual Grade School Math; OpenAI-reported figure, mirrored on this community thread since the original openai.com post blocks automated fetching [relabeled self-reported: aggregator/mirror relay of a vendor figure, not an independent re-measurement]

DROP (F1) score of 83.4 for GPT-4o, part of the same OpenAI-sourced benchmark table (MMLU/GPQA/MATH/HumanEval/MGSM) already used for this model's other 4 rows. Directly fetched this community.openai.com thread and confirmed it literally lists 'DROP: 83.4' alongside the other 5 figures already in the corpus.

MMMU (validation), 0-shot CoT. OpenAI's own figure from the 'Hello GPT-4o' launch blog (openai.com/index/hello-gpt-4o/, blocked to automated fetch, HTTP 403), reproduced verbatim in Table 2 of Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet Model Card Addendum, which cites this column as 'GPT-4o [8]' = OpenAI's Hello GPT-4o post.
MathVista (testmini), 0-shot CoT. Same provenance as the MMMU row: OpenAI's Hello GPT-4o figures, reproduced in Anthropic's Claude 3.5 Sonnet Model Card Addendum Table 2.
AI2D (test), 0-shot. Same provenance as the MMMU row above.
ChartQA (test, relaxed accuracy), 0-shot CoT. Same provenance as the MMMU row above.
DocVQA (test, ANLS score), 0-shot. Same provenance as the MMMU row above.

ORIGINAL (non-Verified) SWE-bench score, deliberately distinct from the 33.2% SWE-bench-Verified figure in the same OpenAI post (no swe-bench-verified taxonomy slug exists -- do not conflate). Verbatim quote from OpenAI's 'Introducing SWE-bench Verified' blog: 'GPT-4o resolves 33.2% of samples on SWE-bench Verified... doubling its previous score of 16% on SWE-bench.' Uses the best-performing open-source agentic scaffold (Agentless) with GPT-4o as the base model, not a bare single-turn completion. The original openai.com/index/introducing-swe-bench-verified/ post returns HTTP 403 to automated fetch; sourced via a GitHub-mirrored copy reproducing the text verbatim (same fallback pattern already used elsewhere in this corpus entry for openai.com-blocked content).

'Correct' % from Table 3 of OpenAI's SimpleQA paper ('Measuring short-form factuality in large language models'). The paper labels the row simply 'GPT-4o' without a dated snapshot; the corresponding F-score for the same row is 38.4.

5-shot CoT, from TIGER-Lab's own MMLU-Pro leaderboard/paper (the benchmark's creators), published June 2024 -- contemporaneous with GPT-4o's initial release. Note: Meta's 'Llama 3 Herd of Models' paper (arXiv 2407.21783) independently re-measured GPT-4o at 74.0 on nominally the same 5-shot-CoT config; used the benchmark authors' own figure here as the more canonical measurement to avoid a conflicting duplicate row.

From Table 2 of Meta's 'The Llama 3 Herd of Models' (arXiv 2407.21783), which independently evaluated GPT-4o as a comparison baseline for the Llama 3.1 release. Verified directly against the paper's PDF table.
MBPP EvalPlus (i.e. MBPP+), 0-shot. From the same Meta Llama-3 paper comparison table (Table 2), verified directly against the PDF.
8-shot CoT. From the same Meta Llama-3 paper comparison table (Table 2), verified directly against the PDF.
0-shot. From the same Meta Llama-3 paper comparison table (Table 2), verified directly against the PDF.

Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard, from the same Meta Llama-3 paper table (published July 23, 2024, before BFCL v3's Sept 2024 launch). Meta reports this as plain 'BFCL' (not a versioned v3 variant), so mapped to the 'bfcl' slug rather than 'bfcl-v3' -- matching this corpus's existing convention for the identical table (see corpus/models/meta/llama-3-1-405b.json's bfcl row).

TAU-bench retail domain, pass^1, gpt-4o with the tool-calling (TC) strategy, from Sierra Research's official leaderboard/repo. Airline-domain score (42.0%, pass^1) omitted per this corpus's established convention of filing only the retail split under the single 'tau-bench' slug to avoid a duplicate-key collision (see corpus/models/anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet-new.json for precedent).

verificationNotes: Release May 13, 2024 confirmed (Wikipedia); 128K context and $2.50/$10 per-million pricing confirmed via multiple independent pricing pages. MMLU 88.7 verified literally on the cited Wikipedia page. However, Wikipedia's GPT-4o article does NOT literally contain the GPQA/MATH/HumanEval/MGSM numbers (directly fetched and confirmed only MMLU is present there) — the original sourceUrl was wrong for those 4 rows. OpenAI's primary announcement (openai.com/index/hello-gpt-4o/) returned HTTP 403 to automated fetch, so I re-sourced those 4 rows to a community.openai.com thread I fetched directly and confirmed contains the exact literal figures (53.6/76.6/90.2/90.5), changing their source type to 'independent'.
